solve(s) ->

            calculate_sum(FreqMap)
    end.

count_frequencies(s) ->
    Counts = [char | s], % Treating list of chars as the input representation
    Map = #{},
    FreqMap = process_counts(Counts, Map),
    {FreqMap, s}.

process_counts([], Map) -> Map;
process_counts([C|Rest], Map) ->
    case maps:get(C, Map) of
        {Count, _} ->
            process_counts(Rest, maps:put(C, {Count + 1, _}, Map));
        _ ->
            process_counts(Rest, maps:put(C, {1, _}, Map))
    end.

calculate_sum(FreqMap) ->
    UniqueChars = maps:keys(FreqMap),
    Sum = calculate_sum_recursive(UniqueChars, FreqMap, 0, #{}),
    Sum.

calculate_sum_recursive([C|Rest], FreqMap, Acc, Processed) ->
    case maps:get(C, FreqMap) of
        {_Freq, _} ->
            MirrorC = get_mirror(C),
            
            case maps:get(MirrorC, FreqMap) of
                {_FreqM, _} when C == MirrorC ->
                    % Self-mirroring character: diff is 0. Mark as processed.
                    NewProcessed = maps:insert(C, true, Processed),
                    NewAcc = Acc + 0,
                    calculate_sum_recursive(Rest, FreqMap, NewAcc, NewProcessed);

                    % Mirror character already processed as 'c' in an earlier pair, skip.
                    calculate_sum_recursive(Rest, FreqMap, Acc, Processed);

                    C_ordered = if C < MirrorC -> C; true -> MirrorC end,

                    if maps:get(C_ordered, Processed) == true ->

byte_to_char(Byte) ->

            else
                % Process pair (CharC, MirrorChar)
                FreqC = maps:get(CharC, FreqMap, 0),
                FreqM = maps:get(MirrorChar, FreqMap, 0),
                Diff = abs(FreqC - FreqM),

                % Mark both as processed
                ProcessedMap' = maps:put(CharC, true, maps:put(MirrorChar, true, ProcessedMap)),
                
                % Recurse on the remaining characters
                Diff + sum_diff(RemainingChars, FreqMap, ProcessedMap')
            end.

%% Helper function to determine the mirror character
get_mirror(Char) ->
    case char_to_ord(Char) of
        % Letter ('a' through 'z')
        CharCode when CodeChar(CharCode) =:= char_to_ord('a') and CodeChar(CharCode) =:= char_to_ord('z') ->
            % a=97, z=122. Sum = 219. Mirror = 219 - Code
            MirrorCode = 219 - CharCode,
            char_from_ord(MirrorCode);
            
        % Digit ('0' through '9')
        CharCode when CodeChar(CharCode) =:= char_to_ord('0') and CodeChar(CharCode) =:= char_to_ord('9') ->
            % 0=48, 9=57. Sum = 105. Mirror = 105 - Code
            MirrorCode = 105 - CharCode,
            char_from_ord(MirrorCode);
            
        _ ->
            % Should not happen based on constraints
            Char
    end.

% Helper to convert character to its ASCII/Unicode code point (integer)
char_to_ord(Char) ->
    case char_to_bits(Char) of
        {Bits, _} -> list_to_integer(Bits)
    end.

% Helper to convert integer code point back to character
char_from_ord(Code) ->
    integer_to_bits(Code, {Bits, _})
end.
